risk_rationale: The Kids Online Safety Act introduces new requirements for default safeguard settings and parental control tools that may necessitate immediate product changes to align with the legislation.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Account privacy for 13-15 years is private by default with no opt-out allowed.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The legislation may require additional safeguard settings beyond account privacy, such as content restrictions or activity monitoring.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Family Pairing availability is globally rolled out.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The legislation may require enhancements to existing parental control tools to meet new standards.

remediations.title: Enhance Default Safeguard Settings
remediations.description: Implement additional default safeguard settings for minors, such as content restrictions and activity monitoring, to comply with the Kids Online Safety Act.
acceptance_criteria: New safeguard settings are implemented and enabled by default for minors
Settings comply with the Kids Online Safety Act requirements

remediations.title: Upgrade Parental Control Tools
remediations.description: Enhance existing parental control tools to provide more comprehensive monitoring and control options for parents, in line with the Kids Online Safety Act.
acceptance_criteria: Parental control tools offer enhanced features as per the new legislation
Tools are user-friendly and accessible to parents

business_impact: Failure to comply with the Kids Online Safety Act could result in significant legal and financial repercussions, including fines and potential restrictions on platform operations in the US market.
recommended_actions: Conduct a detailed gap analysis to identify all areas requiring updates
Prioritize engineering resources to implement necessary changes
Engage with legal and policy teams to ensure full compliance with the legislation
